chapters.summary: The latest episode of the Indie Hackers podcast features an episode on cryptocurrencies. Courtland is convinced that nfts could be the future of the web. But nothing contained in this episode is financial advice. Do your own research.
chapters.gist: Indie Hackers: Cryptocurrency Episode 1
chapters.headline: Courtland is doing an episode on cryptocurrencies, specifically nfts

chapters.summary: Mubasar Iqbal and Heaton Shah talk about their interest in the NFT space. How to make money in crypto? Start with level one. You can make money, but you can also lose a lot of money.
chapters.gist: How to Make Money in Crypto With NFT
chapters.headline: Heat and Shah discuss how to make money investing in decentralized finance

chapters.summary: Nfts lie on this bedrock of a lot more complex tokens and crypto and blockchains. It can be hard to see why they're even new or useful compared to technology we already have. It's hard to understand whether or not these are just a passing fad or worth investing your time and money into.
chapters.gist: In the Elevator With Nefat and Crypto
chapters.headline: Both of you are founders of nfts. It can be hard to understand what nfts are

chapters.summary: Web three is the evolution of how we host and we do infrastructure online. It's just hosted in the cloud, but it's not even a cloud that we own or rent. That fundamentally is what has me interested and why I think it's the future.
chapters.gist: Web 3: The Next Evolution
chapters.headline: Was there ever a time where you were skeptics about nfts

chapters.summary: With the blockchain, what a decentralized ledger is, is it's a decentralized database. For the first time, we're actually building things in this mystical place called the Internet. Why aren't you skeptical about nfts?
chapters.gist: Are We Skeptical About the Blockchain?
chapters.headline: Gary: Blockchain is an entirely different technology from the Internet. On the Internet, it exists across hundreds or thousands of computers

chapters.summary: NFT is a non fungible token. It's essentially digital property. Non fungible means each individual in one is its own distinct thing. The killer use case for this right now is people creating digital items in the form of art and collectibles.
chapters.gist: Non-Fully-Flexible Tokens
chapters.headline: Think about fungible tokens. Bitcoin and ethereum are fungible

chapters.summary: The technology of NFTs enables us to open up the world where if I build a game I can use anybody's NFT inside of my own platform. It's available for public on the ledger with an open API that anybody can access as well. It has huge implications to how e commerce works, how commerce works not just in this online world.
chapters.gist: Will NFTs Change the Way e-Commerce Works?
chapters.headline: NFTs could have huge implications for how e commerce works

chapters.summary: Courtland Heaton: Where do you think things are going in the future? He says the killer use case has to do with ownership. NFTs really do allow ownership. They allow people to put items on the blockchain and to verify who it came from.
chapters.gist: NFT: From Shill to Collectible
chapters.headline: You hosted a Twitter space where people were pitching their NFT projects

chapters.summary: I don't necessarily see a world in which people abandon Twitter or current social networks en masse because they don't own their stuff. NFTs, they're coming in. I can see a path where two, three years from now, companies like Twitter will give you an NFT for every tweet.
chapters.gist: Mugs on the Future of Twitter, Facebook
chapters.headline: I don't necessarily see a world in which people abandon Twitter or current social networks

chapters.summary: Internet visionaries see a future of telecommuting workers and interactive libraries. Do computer pundits lack all common sense? Everything he predicted was wrong. Even if you're very savvy, these futuristic predictions might look inaccurate at the time.
chapters.gist: The Future of the Internet Was Predictions in 1995
chapters.headline: A Newsweek article from 1995 argued against predictions of what the Internet would do

chapters.summary: Keaton: What do you see digital property enabling in the future? Keaton: I think things like sports collectibles and things, again, it's a trend that's already started. How do you know this won't just, like, unravel?
chapters.gist: Future of Digital Property: Possessing Digital Assets
chapters.headline: What do you see digital property enabling in the future

chapters.summary: Aaron Carroll: Can we already do tickets without the blockchain? Carroll: Some of the use cases are kind of obvious, but some are sort of unimaginable. Carroll: It's an enabling technology. It's a crazy idea, but it'll happen.
chapters.gist: On the Blockchain and the Creator Economy
chapters.headline: Can we already do tickets without the blockchain? Do we really need this

chapters.summary: There's a lot of advantages to turning digital items into NFTs. The items you put on the blockchain are portable because the blockchain is publicly accessible. All of their early users basically become investors. And I think there's this trend with the web. It's increasingly about money.
chapters.gist: Are NFTs the Future of App Development?
chapters.headline: There are a lot of advantages to storing digital items on the blockchain

chapters.summary: Chris Dixon: The marketing they do is just not traditional marketing. It's community marketing. The only time that I saw such a tight feedback loop, whether it's nfts or whatever, was clubhouse. The secondary system here on the blockchain that I think is going to flip more stuff upside down.
chapters.gist: Chris Dixon On The Community
chapters.headline: Chris Dixon says cryptocurrency marketing is not traditional marketing

chapters.summary: People aren't going to venture capital to go raise money so they can build a video game. It's the people who want to play it, who actually own the rights to it and will because they believe in it, they can actually share in the upside as well. It is changing who owns what we're going to do for the next five or ten, 2000 years.
chapters.gist: The Fight for the Future of Video Games
chapters.headline: People aren't going to venture capital to go raise money for a video game

chapters.summary: Is there any connection between this fanciful world of nfts and an actual business you're running as a founder? The financial models of the world are going to change in terms of where do we go to borrow money. At some point the model will flip.
chapters.gist: How to Invest in the Crypto World With a SaaS Company
chapters.headline: Let's talk about how you can actually invest in this as a founder today

chapters.summary: I would love to see a world where almost everything is represented as an NFT. That tells me something about the identity of the people who come to my website. It takes time to, because those are old, established industries and technologies. But I think we'll hit there at some point.
chapters.gist: What I Want to See on Founder Path
chapters.headline: I'll tell you what I want to see with founder Path. If a founder goes to founder Path and they raise

chapters.summary: Heaton: What if someone could access a document with their kind of metamask and their crypto credentials instead of their email. If I were a SaaS business, there is this NFT concept where it's like, access and all the stuff. But I don't see how you would want to jam that into your business.
chapters.gist: Will It Affect My SaaS?
chapters.headline: Heaton, you're working on security or redundancy for file storage

chapters.summary: Simple idea with the community is people can tip each other with crypto somehow. Does someone already have a wallet or not? Do they have they already jumped through those hoops? And if they haven't, is it your job to train them?
chapters.gist: What about a Community Crypto Tip?
chapters.headline: Simple idea with community is people can tip each other with crypto somehow

chapters.summary: The only nfc I show, the token drop. I think this concept is called social tokens. It's almost like doing kind of an IPO for a community where you issue tokens to everybody. And I want to do an entire episode on that.
chapters.gist: Social Tokens: The Token Drop
chapters.headline: So let me show you what just happened. Check this out. I registered it for a long time because it's Heaton

chapters.summary: Cortlandt: What next? Is there a particular website they should trust? Find a buddy system in all my Noob spaces. If you're really doing it for savings long term, don't put a lot of money in there.
chapters.gist: Buying Crypto With a Trustful Friend
chapters.headline: Cortlandt: You've said buy crypto, but what next

chapters.summary: All right, Heaton mobs, thanks so much for coming on. Can you let us know where they can go to find out more about what you're up to with your SaaS businesses. For me, I'm on twitter. Everything's linked from there.
chapters.gist: Shirik Bal and Heaton
chapters.headline: All right, Heaton mobs, thanks so much for coming on

episode_title: #235 – An NFT Conversation for Indie Hackers with Hiten Shah and Mubashar Iqbal
